title: 403 Error during Google Drive multipart upload
symptom: When attempting to upload a file to Google Drive using multipart upload, the API sometimes returns a 403 error with the message 'storageQuotaExceeded'.
applies_when: Using Google Drive API for multipart uploads
not_this_if: Using standard JSON body for file creation
No error occurs during GET requests
root_cause: The 403 error indicates that the service account may have exceeded its storage quota or that there are issues with the shared drive's storage limits. This can happen even if the account has permissions, as the quota is a separate limitation.
fix_steps: Check the storage quota for the service account and shared drive.
Ensure that there is sufficient space available for uploads.
agent_instruction: Verify the storage quota and ensure there is enough space in the shared drive.
